title: Research product — one biological object the atlas tracks
description: Per MANAGER_SPEC.md §1. A research product is the named, status-bearing biological object that other atlas layers depend on. Only kind=biological_object products are tracked by the manager; derived_table and intermediate stay at the layer / result level. Status is recomputed live by the manager — do not store it.

kind.description: Only biological_object is tracked by the manager.

atlas.description: Owning atlas (inversion_atlas, relatedness_atlas, ...). Optional for cross-atlas products.

grain.description: Free-form description of the row-level grain ('sample x inversion', 'pair x interval', 'window x metric').

biological_scope.description: What organism / dataset / reference the product is about. Required.

sample_scope.description: Sample-axis reference. Typically '<registry>.<qc_tag>' (e.g. sample_registry.qcpass_226). Free-form for v1.

backed_by_layers.description: Layer_ids that hold the product's data. The manager resolves each via the librarian when computing status.

analysis_id.description: FK into analysis_registry. Empty when the product is curated by hand.

depends_on.description: Other product_ids the product depends on. Walked by the manager for blocked / stale propagation.

valid_for.description: Free-form list of downstream-use tokens. Convention: short snake_case verbs.

confidence.description: Independent of computed status. A product can be 'ready' only when confidence='review_passed'.

path.description: Primary file path for the product (relative to registry root). Optional.
